sub_mechanisms: M_1: middle-thirds removal construction on training-step interval
M_2: LR active on Cantor subintervals, plateau on removed middle thirds
M_3: recursion depth d controls fractal granularity
stripped_llm_application: Build the training-step LR schedule from a recursive interval-removal construction: split [0,T] into thirds, remove the middle third, recurse on the outer thirds to depth d. LR decays on the surviving subintervals; LR is held constant during the removed middle thirds. Step count and decay rate scale with the self-similar dimension log2/log3.
novelty_claim_stripped: Cosine / WSD / Decoupled Relative LR are smooth or piecewise-smooth schedules; the Agarwal-Goel-Zhang fractal LR uses Chebyshev-derived step ordering. This candidate uses an EXPLICIT Cantor middle-thirds construction with depth-d recursion.
